name: Abd al-Rahman ibn Abd Rabbah
title: Narrator of Ghadir
shortDescription: A companion of the Prophet (PBUH) and witness to the event of Ghadir Khumm who prepared for martyrdom in Karbala with great enthusiasm.
fullStory:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Abd Rabbah al-Ansari was a noble companion of the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) and a sincere supporter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). He was an eyewitness to the event of Ghadir Khumm. Later in Kufa, when Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) asked the people in the Rahba square to testify if they had heard the hadith of Ghadir, Abd al-Rahman stood up and testified decisively.

When Imam Hussain (AS) went to Mecca, Abd al-Rahman traveled from Kufa to Mecca and accompanied the Imam all the way to Karbala. On the night of Ashura, he was in a very remarkably joyful and happy spirit.

Burayr ibn Khudhayr, seeing him laughing and joking, said: 'O Abd al-Rahman, this is not the time for jokes and laughter!'. Abd al-Rahman replied with a loving smile: 'My people know that I was not a joker in my youth, let alone in my old age; but I laugh now because I know that tomorrow, there is no distance between us and Paradise except the sword strikes of these people'.

On the day of Ashura, this brave and loving old man entered the battlefield after getting permission from Imam Hussain (AS). Despite his old age, he was a skilled swordsman and kept the memory of the Prophet (PBUH) and Ali (AS) alive in the field with his war poetry.

In a heroic battle, after killing a group of enemies, Abd al-Rahman sacrificed his life defending the son of the one for whose father the Prophet had taken allegiance at Ghadir Khumm, and joined the ranks of the martyrs of Karbala.
quoteArabic: وَاللَّهِ ما بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَ الحُورِ العِينِ إِلاّ أَنْ يَميلَ هؤُلاءِ عَلَيْنا بِأَسْيافِهِمْ
quote: By God, there is no distance between us and Paradise except the sword strikes of these people.
